content: intern training plan ai engineering track february 16 april 27 2026 duration 10 weeks 1 training overview objective equip interns with the practical python skills llm framework expertise langchain langgraph and ai library proficiency needed to build productiongrade intelligent document processing and agentic ai workflows training approach each phase follows a structured learn practice build model interns complete handson exercises miniprojects and code reviews at the end of each phase daily standups and weekly demos ensure accountability and progress tracking key principles learn by doing every concept is paired with a handson exercise git from day 1 all work is committed to repositories from the start production mindset testing logging error handling and security are not optional code reviews peer reviews and mentor feedback on every deliverable 2 training timeline at a glance 3 phase 1 python for langchain langgraph level 200 duration february 16 march 1 2026 2 weeks goal ensure interns can comfortably build llm workflows agents and pipelines only python topics directly relevant to aillm development are covered note interns set up git repositories on day 1 all code is committed daily from the start week 1 feb 16 feb 22 core python oop pydantic day 12 core python refresh environment setup environment setup python 312 vs code git init gitignore virtual environments venvconda pip requirementstxt dependency management pythondotenv managing api keys and secrets via env files securely functions defaultkeyword args argskwargs lambda functions comprehensions list dict and set comprehensions for data transformations error handling tryexceptfinally custom exceptions raising errors modules packages import system relative vs absolute imports initpy modular coding splitting logic across files creating reusable utility modules exercise build a modular text processing utility across 3 files with proper imports error handling and a env config file for settings push to git day 34 objectoriented programming critical for langchain why langchain tools agents chains and callbacks all use oop patterns understanding classes is nonnegotiable classes objects attributes methods init self inheritance extending base classes super method resolution order mro composition vs inheritance when to use each pattern method overriding customizing behavior in child classes magic methods str repr call len abstract base classes abc defining interfaces and contracts structuring reusable components building toolchainlike class hierarchies exercise create a mini agent framework with a base agent class specialized agents searchagent calculatoragent using inheritance and a router that picks the right agent day 5 type hints pydantic essential for langchainlanggraph why langchain uses pydantic everywhere structured outputs tool schemas inputoutput models langgraph state schemas require typeddict this is nonnegotiable type annotations basic types optional union list dict tuple typeddict defining typed dictionary schemas used in langgraph state pydantic basemodel defining data models with validation field validators custom validation logic field constraints nested models composing complex schemas from simpler ones json serialization modeldump modeljsonschema practical patterns using pydantic for api response parsing config management exercise define pydantic models for a document processing pipeline documentinput processingconfig extractionresult add validators and serialize to json week 2 feb 23 mar 1 async apis data testing cli day 67 async programming very important for llm pipelines why llm api calls are iobound async enables concurrent execution dramatically speeding up multistep ai pipelines asyncawait basics coroutines event loop concepts asyncio gather createtask running multiple coroutines concurrently async http calls httpxasyncclient for parallel api requests async patterns semaphores for rate limiting timeouts cancellation error handling in async code tryexcept in coroutines exception groups exercise build an async script that calls 3 public apis simultaneously collects results and merges them into json compare sync vs async execution time day 8 working with apis rest api fundamentals get post headers status codes query params requests httpx libraries sync and async http clients json response handling parsing navigating nested structures api authentication api keys bearer tokens basic auth env for secrets rate limiting retry logic backoff strategies retry decorators working with llm provider apis openai anthropic azure openai api structure exercise build a wrapper class for an llm api that handles auth retries error handling and response parsing using pydantic models day 9 data handling text processing json readwrite jsonload jsondump working with nested data csv handling csv module dictreaderdictwriter yamltoml config files reading configuration from structured files document processing reading txt pdf pypdf2 docx pythondocx text preprocessing cleaning tokenizing normalizing text for rag pipelines exercise build a document ingestion pipeline read mixed documents from a folder extract text clean it output structured json with metadata filename word count type day 10 testing logging cli tools why testing production ai code must be tested mocking llm api calls is essential to avoid costs during development testing pytest fundamentals test functions assertions fixtures parametrize mocking unittestmock patching api calls mocking llm responses test organization test directory structure conftestpy naming conventions logging debugging logging module levels formatters file handlers structured logging debugging pipelines breakpoints pdb ide debuggers exception tracing reading tracebacks traceback module cli tools argparse building commandline interfaces for scripts running scripts as tools entry points script arguments exercise create a cli tool argparse that processes files from a folder logs progress and includes pytest tests with mocked file operations package with requirementstxt phase 1 deliverable assessment miniproject smart document processor a clibased python application that accepts a folder of mixed documents via argparse uses oop with a base processor class and formatspecific subclasses validates inputs and outputs using pydantic models processes files asynchronously for performance calls a public api for text enrichment eg language detection outputs structured json results with comprehensive logging includes pytest test suite with mocked api calls stored in a git repository with proper commits and gitignore 4 phase 2 langchain fundamentals duration march 2 march 15 2026 2 weeks goal master langchains core abstractions and build functional llmpowered applications with proper evaluation and security awareness week 3 mar 2 mar 8 core langchain week 4 mar 9 mar 15 rag agents production patterns phase 2 deliverable build a ragbased qa system that ingests documents creates a vector store and answers questions with source citations include custom tools conversation memory pydantic output schemas langsmith tracing response caching and a test suite 5 phase 3 langgraph multiagent flows duration march 16 march 29 2026 2 weeks goal build stateful multistep ai workflows using langgraphs graphbased architecture week 5 mar 16 mar 22 langgraph fundamentals week 6 mar 23 mar 29 advanced patterns phase 3 deliverable build a multiagent document processing system using langgraph with a router agent extraction agent summarization agent and qa agent include conditional routing state management humanintheloop approval and persistence add evaluation metrics for output quality 6 phase 4 document ai fastapi integrations duration march 30 april 12 2026 2 weeks goal gain handson experience with document ai libraries and learn to serve ai workflows as production apis using fastapi week 7 mar 30 apr 5 document ai embeddings week 8 apr 6 apr 12 fastapi observability integration why fastapi building an ai agent is only half the job engineers must expose agentschains as rest apis fastapi is the standard in the langchain ecosystem phase 4 deliverable build an intelligent document processing api a fastapi service that accepts document uploads performs ocr extracts structured data using llm generates embeddings and enables semantic search include observability with langsmithlangfuse tracing 7 phase 5 advanced git team workflow emergingtech pocs duration april 13 april 19 2026 1 week goal master collaborative git workflows complete a capstone project that integrates all skills from phases 14 note basic git init add commit push was introduced in phase 1 day 1 this phase covers advanced collaboration workflows days 12 advanced git team workflow days 35 capstone project capstone endtoend ai document intelligence system interns work as a team using proper git workflow to build a complete system that demonstrates mastery across all phases python backend with oop design pydantic models async processing phase 1 langchain rag pipeline with prompt engineering and custom tools phase 2 langgraph multiagent workflow with conditional routing and state management phase 3 document ingestion with ocr served via fastapi with observability phase 4 collaborative development using feature branches prs and code reviews phase 5 presentation each intern presents their contribution and demonstrates the system on the final day 10 tools environment setup 11 recommended learning phase 1 preparation assessment open ended scenarios set 1 architecture oop the skeleton 1 explain why you would use an abstract base class for a document processor that needs to handle both pdf and docx files 2 your baseprocessor has a process method how do you implement polymorphism to ensure that a pdfprocessor and a docxprocessor can both be called using the same process syntax 3 why is composition preferred over deep inheritance chains when building agentic tools 4 you are splitting your code into modelspy utilspy and processorpy describe the directory structure and why an initpy file is necessary for these imports to work 5 how do you use str or repr to improve the debuggability of your document objects 6 explain the purpose of superinit when a child class adds extra configuration to a base class 7 if you have two parent classes that both have a method called cleanup how does pythons mro determine which one is called when you run selfcleanup 8 why is it a security risk to store api keys directly as class attributes in your source code 9 explain the difference between a classmethod and an instance method in the context of creating a new processor instance 10 if you encounter a circular import error between two modules what is the best architectural way to resolve it 11 describe how to use args and kwargs to make your base process function forward custom settings to child classes 12 why do we define private attributes using an underscore prefix attribute and what is the community consensus on accessing them 13 explain the benefit of using a dataclass for a data only structure versus a standard class 14 in your project how would you design a factory to return the correct processor based on a files extension 15 why does the use of self in methods make oop code more manageable than standard global functions 16 explain what method resolution order is in plain english 17 what happens if you define a method in a child class that has the exact same name as one in the parent class 18 when building an agent why is it better for an agent to have a tool composition rather than to be a tool inheritance 19 how do you implement a property that acts like a variable but runs logic every time it is accessed 20 why do we keep a requirementstxt file and what is the risk of not updating it as you add new libraries set 2 pydantic async pipelines the data speed 1 a user provides a document with a missing wordcount explain how a pydantic basemodel handles this compared to standard python 2 why is asynciogather faster than using a for loop to call an enrichment api for 50 files 3 explain what happens if you use timesleep inside an async function 4 how does a pydantic field with minlength or gt constraint help prevent dirty data from reaching your llm 5 what is the specific danger of using a mutable default argument like an empty list in your model 6 explain how asynciosemaphore prevents you from being banned by an api provider for too many requests 7 when using httpxasyncclient why must you use it within an async with block 8 if an api request takes too long what asyncio function do you use to give up on that specific request after 5 seconds 9 how does typeddict differ from a pydantic basemodel when defining state in a langgraph workflow 10 describe the step bystep process of converting a pydantic object into a json string 11 what is the benefit of returnexceptionstrue in asynciogather 12 explain how to use configdict to allow extra fields in an api response that arent in your schema 13 why is optionalstr preferred over str for fields that are not guaranteed to be present in a document 14 how do you use defaultfactory to generate a dynamic timestamp for every new document model 15 explain why standard requests is generally avoided in production grade async pipelines 16 what is the difference between a coroutine and a regular function and how does the keyword await signal a yield point 17 how does a custom validator in pydantic allow you to clean text eg stripping whitespace before it is assigned to a field 18 why would you use asyncioshield on a critical data writing task 19 in a rag pipeline why is it better to chunk your document text before sending it to an llm 20 explain the role of the event loop when you have 10 concurrent api calls running set 3 production mindset testing cli 1 why is it considered a bad practice to hit a real api during a unit test 2 describe how you would use unittestmock to simulate a successful api response for your enrichment tool 3 how does conftestpy simplify the setup of shared test data across different test files 4 explain the difference between pytest fixtures and standard setup functions 5 why is it important to use argparse instead of hard coding the file path in your mainpy 6 how do you implement a verbose flag in argparse that prints debug logs when present 7 why is a logging hierarchy eg logginggetloggername better than simple print calls 8 if your script crashes in production what specific information should your log file contain to help you find the bug 9 explain the benefit of running pytest cov on your code 10 what is a mocking strategy for an api call that returns a 404 or a 500 error 11 why do we keep env files in gitignore 12 how do you handle a scenario where a user provides a directory that does not exist using argparse 13 what is the significance of using pytestmarkasyncio on a test function 14 if you have a test that passes in your ide but fails in a cicd environment what is the most likely cause 15 why should your document processor use a tryexcept block for file reading operations 16 explain what parameterization is in pytest and why you would use it for testing multiple file formats 17 what should be the first thing a developer does after cloning a project repository regarding dependencies 18 why is code review a vital step for an intern even if the code works perfectly 19 how do you structure your logs so they can be easily read by an external log aggregator like datadog or elk 20 why is it important to define a clear main entry point in your application

identify if there is an insurance on historical monumentreligious places what type of insurance and who insured them any new type or rare type of insurance insurance fundamentals aggregator who compares different policies distributor who provides their own policies 1 insurance risk transfer mechanism policy holderinsured insurer insurance company 2 pooling concept bringing similar type of risks together ex an auto insurance company provides auto insurances of all types and they might have sub pools ex maharashtra telangana etc or age groups 3 law of large numbers when a large number of similar risks are grouped together the average outcome becomes more predictable 4 risk possibility of loss 41 pure risk something which is definitely going to damage if it has to happen 42 speculative risk ex mutual funds which can give us gains or loss 5 peril reason behind the loss ex damage to house due to fire peril fire 51 open perils most of the coverages will be covered except exclusions 52 named perils only for specific perils the loss will be covered 10022026 risk management process 1 identifying loss exposures identify the properties to loss peril 2 analyzing loss exposures risk peril and hazard 1 hazard is something that increases the likelihood of loss physical moral and morale
